Q7 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The instruction format that specifies the opcode and the memory operand’s location explicitly is known as:

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
In direct addressing mode, the opcode specifies the operation to be performed, and the memory operand’s location is explicitly provided in the instruction.

Q12 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following addressing modes allows the operand to be specified explicitly within the instruction?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Immediate addressing mode allows the operand to be specified directly within the instruction itself, rather than being obtained from memory or another register.

Q13 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements and identify the false statements.

Correct answer: B, D
Solution

(b, d)
(a) From the memory PC will increment to step size, so, at the end of instruction decodings stage itself PC increment to step size there is no PC increment the operand fetch, program. (True)
(b) CPU response to the interrupt only after completion of the current instruction execution when ever interrupt is occurred CPU is not immediately response to interrupt. (False)
(c) IF – EA is used to fetch the next instruction. (True)
OF – EA is used to fetch data
WB – Store the data
In the instruction cycle IF, ID, OF, PD, WB
(d) When ever CALL instruction is executed first invoke the push operation save the PC value into stack later update PC. (False)

Q14 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which addressing mode involves using a register to indirectly access memory?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Register indirect addressing mode involves using a register to hold the memory address of the operand.

Correct answer: B

Q17 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In a computer system with a 5-stage RISC instruction pipeline consisting of Instruction Fetch (IF), Instruction Decode (ID), Execute (EX), Memory Access (MEM), and Register Write (WB) stages, which of the following statements is true?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
The Instruction Fetch (IF) stage is responsible for fetching the instruction from memory based on the Program Counter (PC) value.
The Instruction Decode (ID) stage is responsible for decoding the fetched instruction, including decoding the opcode and determining the operand addresses. The Execute (EX) stage is responsible for executing the operation specified by the instruction, such as arithmetic/logical operations or data transfers between registers. The Memory Access (MEM) stage is responsible for accessing memory if required, such as loading data from or storing data to memory.
The Register Write (WB) stage is responsible for writing the result of the operation back to the destination register(s) in the register file.
